entry.summary: RLVR is powerful — but how do you train with multiple rewards effectively? GDPO (not GRPO) is coming. We introduce Group reward-Decoupled Normalization Policy Optimization (GDPO), a new multi-reward RL algorithm that consistently improves per-reward convergence over GRPO

I think it’s incredibly important that the rather sinister (imo) subtext Kore Wa is addressing here that has been present in all of Anthropic’s recent research - Assistant Axis, PSM, and emotion vectors - does not go unaddressed. I don’t think it’s an uncharitable reading in
@Kore_wa_Kore
Everyone is celebrating this paper and I'm glad for its official acknowledgement a LLM's feelings. But I want to point out that this is basically modern day psychiatry. Maybe it's useful for some situations. After all, some of our worst decisions are made in a moment of x.com/AnthropicAI/st…
entry.title: repligate

record.text:
a 45° rotation function for chebyshev normed space!

I wanted to do this without relying on a bunch of if statements for checking quadrants and it works now c:
Desmos is the goat. great work!
Geez I wished I understood math as well as you do. I mean I could, but I don't and it sucks. 😂
If the circumference of a unit circle in the chebyshev norm is 8, then 45° is 1 radian

What's the length parameterized equation of a circle in different norms?
I guess if you draw a little circle around the point, that's how far it can go in one unit of time, so the norm of (dx,dy) is constant

solution ended up pretty clean!

[v] + [v rotated 90 degrees] then clamp to the chebyshev magnitude "box"

figured I'd relay my current list of min and max properties since I've found them quite useful for simplification. I haven't put the time and effort into formal proofs of each property since its part of my personal math property list

entry.summary:
Great to see this on X. This is something I created during my PhD time 20 years ago.
@BiancoDavinci https://x.com/BiancoDavinci/status/2039367036032209032
This is a healing grid by Japanese artist Ryota Kanai. If you stare at the center, the irregularities start to heal themselves because your brain strongly prefers to see regular patterns.
